project.title: LeafLens: Classifying Schoolyard Leaves with a Small Vision Model

summary.headline: Three claims need stronger evidence before LeafLens can support its conclusion.
summary.nextStep: Defend each finding, revise the method, and hand the mastery receipt to a mentor.

anchors.excerpt: We used macro-F1 because the six species were not perfectly balanced. The final macro-F1 was 0.91, compared with 0.18 for the majority-class baseline and 0.74 for the color-histogram logistic-regression baseline.
To make the model robust to camera orientation, we created one 90-degree rotated copy of every photograph. We then randomly assigned 80% of the resulting images to training and 20% to testing.

images, labels = load_leaf_photos("data/leaves")
rotated_images = np.stack([np.rot90(image) for image in images])
images = np.concatenate([images, rotated_images])
labels = np.concatenate([labels, labels])

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    images,
    labels,
    test_size=0.20,
    shuffle=True,
)
These results show that LeafLens is better than both simpler alternatives and generalizes to previously unseen leaves.

LeafLens classifies six common tree species from 480 phone photographs. Our model achieved a macro-F1 score of 0.91 on the held-out test set and outperformed both a majority-class baseline and a color-histogram logistic-regression baseline.

model = train_leafnet(X_train, y_train, epochs=20)
predictions = model.predict(X_test).argmax(axis=1)

macro_f1 = accuracy_score(y_test, predictions)
print(f"LeafLens macro-F1: {macro_f1:.2f}")
anchors.deterministicCheck: No baseline fit, prediction, or score call exists anywhere in student-analysis.py.

anchors.excerpt: The final notebook was run on a school laptop. The exact random seed, package versions, and train/test image IDs were not saved.

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    images,
    labels,
    test_size=0.20,
    shuffle=True,
)
anchors.deterministicCheck: The train_test_split call has no random_state argument.

findings.title: The paper reports macro-F1, but the code calculates accuracy
findings.evidenceSummary: The analysis imports and calls accuracy_score, then labels that value as macro-F1.
findings.codeSummary: accuracy_score and macro-averaged F1 are different metrics; the current variable name does not change the calculation.
findings.whyItMatters: Accuracy can hide weak performance on less common species, while macro-F1 gives each species equal weight. The reported number therefore does not support the metric claim.

coach.progressiveHint: Trace the function that produces the number, not the variable name used to print it.
coach.evidenceBackedCorrection: Calculate f1_score(y_test, predictions, average="macro"), rerun the test, and replace every reported score with the resulting value.

findings.title: Augmented copies can cross from training into testing
findings.claim: The score measures generalization to previously unseen leaves.
findings.evidenceSummary: Every image is rotated before the combined pool is randomly split, with no physical-leaf grouping.
findings.codeSummary: An original image and its rotated derivative can be assigned to opposite sides of the split, and multiple photographs of one physical leaf can also cross the boundary.
findings.whyItMatters: The test set is no longer independent of training data, so the score can reward recognition of the same source leaf instead of generalization to a new leaf.

coach.progressiveHint: Ask what real-world object must remain entirely on one side of the train/test boundary.
coach.evidenceBackedCorrection: Split by physical leaf ID first, keep all photos from one leaf in one partition, and create augmented images only from the training partition.

findings.title: The superiority claim has no implemented comparison
findings.claim: LeafLens outperformed majority-class and logistic-regression baselines.
findings.evidenceSummary: The manuscript gives two baseline scores, but the submitted code only trains and scores LeafLens.
findings.codeSummary: A deterministic scan finds no baseline model, majority predictor, baseline prediction, or baseline metric call in the analysis file.
findings.whyItMatters: Without a reproducible comparison under the same split and metric, a reader cannot check whether the claimed improvement exists.

coach.progressiveHint: Look for the training, prediction, and scoring steps for each named comparison model.
coach.evidenceBackedCorrection: Implement both baselines, evaluate all models on the identical held-out leaf IDs with macro-F1, and narrow the claim to the comparisons actually run.

findings.title: The exact split and environment cannot be reproduced
findings.claim: The reported test result can be independently checked.
findings.evidenceSummary: The paper says the seed, package versions, and image-ID split were not saved.
findings.codeSummary: train_test_split is called without random_state, so a new run can select a different test set.
findings.whyItMatters: A different random split may produce a different score, and a mentor cannot reconstruct which images supported the claim.

coach.progressiveHint: List the information another student would need to recreate exactly the same train/test result.
coach.evidenceBackedCorrection: Set and record the random seed, save the leaf-ID split manifest, pin package versions, and document the command used to reproduce the scores.

cleanup.details: Prepared fixture files are public sample data and were not uploaded to OpenAI.

provenance.disclosure: This is a deterministic cached demonstration, not a live model review. External links were curated into the fixture and are not represented as native web-search results.
